question_text: A magnetic force pulls a steel ball towards a magnet held above it. Compare this force with the gravitational force acting on the ball. Which statement is most accurate?

options.label: A
options.text: Both are contact forces; the magnet must touch the ball to exert a force

options.label: B
options.text: Both are non-contact forces, but magnetic force acts through a magnetic field whilst gravitational force acts through a gravitational field

options.label: C
options.text: Magnetic force is non-contact but gravitational force is contact because the Earth is in contact with objects

options.label: D
options.text: Gravitational force is non-contact, but magnetic force is contact because it requires magnetism to be present

correct_answer: Both are non-contact forces, but magnetic force acts through a magnetic field whilst gravitational force acts through a gravitational field
explanation: Both magnetic and gravitational forces are non-contact forces because they can act over a distance without the objects touching. Magnetic force is produced by a magnetic field around a magnet, while gravitational force is produced by a gravitational field around any mass. This question tests understanding that forces can be exerted through invisible fields rather than by direct contact.
